data.title: Critical Analysis of Designer's Perspectives of E-learning Systems for African Learners: Toward User-Centered and Inclusive Designs
data.authors: Grace Ataguba, Oladapo Oyebode, Gerry Chan, Fidelia Orji, Victoria Yemi-Peters, George Frempong, Rita Orji
data.book: Proceedings of the 5th Biennial African Human Computer Interaction Conference
data.description: E-learning systems have gained interest in their adoption in Africa since the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting both opportunities and challenges. This study explores designers' perspectives on developing e-learning systems for African learners, utilizing YouTube as a platform for insights into their approaches and methodologies.  Based on our analysis of 143 relevant YouTube posts, we uncover insights into unique challenges in the African educational landscape, including limited technological infrastructure, diverse linguistic and cultural backgrounds, and varying digital literacy levels. Findings from this study revealed potential for user-centered and inclusive designs that impacts on learning experiences and outcomes of African learners, educational support from stakeholders, and opportunities for affordable systems. Our findings contribute to the human-computer interaction community by identifying prospective …
